ex.en: She has a brilliant mind for mathematics.
ex.ko: 그녀는 수학에 뛰어난 두뇌를 가지고 있다.

ex.en: That's a brilliant idea!
The brilliant sunshine lit up the beach.

budget.en: plan how to spend a particular amount of money

ex.en: We need to budget carefully for the trip.
ex.ko: 우리는 그 여행을 위해 신중하게 예산을 세워야 한다.

budget.en: an amount of money available for spending, planned in advance

ex.en: The company has a large budget for marketing.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 마케팅에 많은 예산을 가지고 있다.

ex.en: They stayed at a budget hotel near the airport.
ex.ko: 그들은 공항 근처의 저가 호텔에 묵었다.

bug.en: a small insect, especially one that causes damage or is unpleasant

ex.en: There's a bug crawling on the wall.

bug.en: a fault or defect in a computer program

ex.en: The new update fixed several bugs.
Stop bugging me about the deadline.

bulb.en: a glass object that produces light when connected to electricity

ex.en: The bulb burned out and needs replacing.

bulb.en: a rounded underground part of a plant from which it grows

ex.en: She planted tulip bulbs in the garden.

burn.en: be destroyed by fire, or destroy something with fire

ex.en: The old barn burned to the ground.

burn.en: an injury to the skin caused by fire, heat, or the sun

ex.en: She got a bad burn from the hot pan.
ex.ko: 그녀는 뜨거운 팬에 심한 화상을 입었다.

burn.en: use fuel or energy to produce power

ex.en: This car burns a lot of gas.

bury.en: put a dead body in the ground

ex.en: They buried him in the family cemetery.

bury.en: put something under the ground or cover it so it cannot be seen

ex.en: The dog buried a bone in the yard.

bury.en: try hard not to think about a painful feeling or memory

ex.en: She tried to bury her sadness and move on.

bus station.en: a place where buses start and end their journeys

ex.en: The buses leave from the bus station every hour.
ex.ko: 버스는 한 시간마다 버스 터미널에서 출발한다.

bus stop.en: a place on the street where buses stop for passengers to get on and off

ex.en: She waited at the bus stop for ten minutes.

bush.en: a low plant with many thin branches

ex.en: They hid behind a bush.

bush.cue: 사람이 살지 않는 야생 지역 (호주 등)
bush.en: wild land that is not farmed or lived in, especially in Australia or Africa

ex.en: They went camping in the bush.

businessman.en: a man who works in business, especially at a high level

ex.en: He is a successful businessman.

businesswoman.en: a woman who works in business, especially at a high level

ex.en: She became a well-known businesswoman.

cab.en: a car with a driver that you pay to take you somewhere

ex.en: We took a cab to the airport.

cab.en: the part of a truck or train where the driver sits

ex.en: The driver climbed into the cab of the truck.

cafeteria.en: a restaurant, often in a school or workplace, where you serve yourself

ex.en: Students eat lunch in the cafeteria.

calendar.en: a chart showing the days, weeks, and months of a year

ex.en: She hung a new calendar on the wall.

calendar.en: a list of events or things planned for particular days

ex.en: Check my calendar before you schedule the meeting.
ex.ko: 회의 일정을 잡기 전에 내 일정표를 확인해 줘.

camping.en: the activity of living outdoors in a tent for a period of time

ex.en: We went camping in the mountains last summer.

campus.en: the buildings and land of a university or college

ex.en: The university campus is very large.

capital.en: the main city of a country, where the government is based

ex.en: Paris is the capital of France.

capital.en: a large amount of money used to start or invest in a business

ex.en: The startup needed more capital to grow.
ex.ko: 그 스타트업은 성장하기 위해 더 많은 자본이 필요했다.

capital.cue: 문장·이름 맨 앞에 쓰는 큰 글자
capital.en: a large letter such as A, B, C, used at the start of a sentence or name

ex.en: Write your name in capitals.

capital letter.cue: 문장 맨 앞에 쓰는 큰 글자
capital letter.en: a large letter such as A, B, C, used at the beginning of a sentence or a name

ex.en: Every sentence should begin with a capital letter.

captain.en: the person in charge of a ship

ex.en: The captain steered the ship through the storm.

captain.en: the leader of a sports team

ex.en: She was chosen as team captain this year.

captain.en: a military rank above lieutenant and below major

ex.en: He was promoted to captain after five years.

car park.en: an area or building where cars can be left for a period of time

ex.en: Leave your car in the car park near the entrance.
ex.ko: 입구 근처 주차장에 차를 세워 두세요.

carrot.en: a long orange vegetable that grows underground

ex.en: Rabbits love eating carrots.

cash.en: money in the form of coins and bills

ex.en: I don't have enough cash to pay for this.

ex.ko: 그는 월급 수표를 현금으로 바꾸러 은행에 갔다.

cassette.en: a flat plastic case containing magnetic tape used for recording or playing music

ex.en: He still listens to music on old cassette tapes.
ex.ko: 그는 아직도 오래된 카세트테이프로 음악을 듣는다.

castle.en: a large, often old, building built to protect the people inside from attack

ex.en: The king lived in a huge castle.

castle.en: in chess, a piece that moves in straight lines, also called a rook

ex.en: She moved her castle across the board.

cause.en: an aim or principle that a group of people support or fight for

ex.en: They raised money for a good cause.
Heavy rain caused the flood.
Smoking is a major cause of lung disease.

cent.en: a small unit of money, one hundredth of a dollar

ex.en: This candy costs fifty cents.

center.en: the middle point or part of something

ex.en: They stood in the center of the room.

center.en: a building or place used for a particular activity

ex.en: She works out at the fitness center every day.

center.en: have something as the main focus or subject

ex.en: The discussion centered on climate change.
ex.ko: 그 논의는 기후 변화에 중점을 두었다.

centimeter.en: a metric unit of length equal to one hundredth of a meter

ex.en: The paper is 30 centimeters long.
The castle was built in the 12th century.

cereal.en: a breakfast food made from grain, often eaten with milk

ex.en: He ate a bowl of cereal for breakfast.
ex.ko: 그는 아침으로 시리얼 한 그릇을 먹었다.

cereal.en: a grass grown for its edible grain, such as wheat or rice

ex.en: Wheat is an important cereal crop.
I am certain that she will come.

certain.en: used to refer to a specific thing or person without naming it exactly

ex.en: Certain foods can cause allergies.
ex.ko: 어떤 음식들은 알레르기를 일으킬 수 있다.

certainly.en: used to emphasize that something is definitely true, or as a strong way of saying yes

ex.en: Certainly, I'll help you with that.

chain.en: a series of connected metal rings

ex.en: The dog was tied with a chain.

chain.en: a group of stores, restaurants, or hotels owned by the same company

ex.en: That coffee chain has shops all over the city.
ex.ko: 그 커피 체인점은 도시 곳곳에 매장이 있다.

chain.en: a series of things or events that are connected

ex.en: A chain of events led to the accident.

chairman.en: the person in charge of a meeting or organization

ex.en: He was elected chairman of the committee.

challenge.en: say that you think something is wrong, untrue, or unfair

ex.en: She challenged the referee's decision.

challenge.en: a new or difficult task that tests your ability

ex.en: Learning a new language is a big challenge.
ex.ko: 새로운 언어를 배우는 것은 큰 도전이다.

challenge.en: invite someone to compete against you

ex.en: He challenged his friend to a game of chess.
ex.ko: 그는 친구에게 체스 게임을 하자고 도전했다.

champagne.en: a white sparkling wine, often drunk to celebrate

ex.en: They opened a bottle of champagne to celebrate.
ex.ko: 그들은 축하하기 위해 샴페인 한 병을 땄다.

championship.en: a competition to find the best player or team in a sport

ex.en: Our team won the championship this year.
ex.ko: 우리 팀은 올해 선수권 대회에서 우승했다.

chance.cue: 무언가를 할 수 있는 좋은 때

ex.en: This is your chance to prove yourself.

chance.en: the possibility that something will happen

ex.en: There's a good chance of rain tomorrow.

chance.en: take a risk in the hope of a good result

ex.en: I decided to chance it and invest my savings.
ex.ko: 나는 위험을 무릅쓰고 저축한 돈을 투자하기로 했다.

chapter.en: one of the main parts into which a book is divided

ex.en: Please read chapter three for homework.

chapter.en: a distinct period in someone's life or in history

ex.en: Moving abroad opened a new chapter in her life.
ex.ko: 해외로 이주한 것은 그녀 인생의 새로운 장을 열었다.

chart.en: show information in the form of a chart or graph

ex.en: The report charts the company's growth over five years.
ex.ko: 그 보고서는 5년간 회사의 성장을 도표로 나타낸다.

ex.en: They charted a course of action for the project.
ex.ko: 그들은 그 프로젝트를 위한 행동 계획을 세웠다.

chart.en: a diagram or graph that shows information

ex.en: The chart shows sales for each month.

chart.en: a list showing which songs are most popular

ex.en: The song reached number one on the charts.

chat.en: talk with someone in a friendly, informal way

ex.en: We chatted about our weekend plans.
ex.ko: 우리는 주말 계획에 대해 담소를 나눴다.

ex.en: Let's have a chat over coffee.
These shoes were really cheap.
The toy broke quickly because it was cheap.
He's too cheap to buy her a proper gift.
ex.ko: 그는 너무 인색해서 그녀에게 제대로 된 선물을 사주지 않는다.

cheek.en: either side of the face below the eyes

ex.en: She kissed him on the cheek.

cheek.en: rude or disrespectful behavior, especially when done confidently

ex.en: He had the cheek to ask for a raise after being late all week.
ex.ko: 그는 일주일 내내 지각하고도 뻔뻔하게 월급 인상을 요구했다.

cheer.en: shout to show support or approval

ex.en: The crowd cheered as the team scored.

cheer.en: make someone feel happier or more hopeful

ex.en: She tried to cheer up her friend after the bad news.
ex.ko: 그녀는 나쁜 소식을 들은 친구를 격려하려 했다.

cheer.en: a shout of approval or joy

ex.en: A loud cheer went up from the fans.

chef.en: a professional cook, especially the head cook of a restaurant

ex.en: The chef prepared a special dish for the guests.
ex.ko: 셰프는 손님들을 위해 특별한 요리를 준비했다.

chemical.en: a substance produced by or used in a chemical reaction

ex.en: The factory produces various chemicals.
The chemical reaction released a lot of heat.
ex.ko: 그 화학 반응은 많은 열을 방출했다.

cheque.en: a printed form used to make a payment from your bank account

ex.en: He paid by cheque.

chess.en: a board game for two players played with 16 pieces each

ex.en: He plays chess with his grandfather every Sunday.

chest.en: the front part of the body between the neck and the stomach

ex.en: He held his hand over his chest.

chest.en: a piece of furniture with drawers for storing clothes

ex.en: She keeps her sweaters in the chest of drawers.

chest.en: a large box with a lid, used for storage

ex.ko: 그들은 다락방에서 오래된 보물 상자를 발견했다.

childhood.en: the time of a person's life when they are a child

ex.en: She spent her childhood in a small village.
ex.ko: 그녀는 어린 시절을 작은 마을에서 보냈다.

chili.en: a small, very hot pepper used to add spicy flavor to food

ex.en: She added chopped chili to the sauce.

chili.en: a spicy dish made with ground meat, beans, and chili peppers

ex.ko: 우리는 저녁으로 칠리 한 그릇을 먹었다.

chimpanzee.en: an intelligent ape that lives in the forests of Africa

ex.en: The chimpanzee used a stick to get the food.
ex.ko: 그 침팬지는 막대기를 사용해 먹이를 얻었다.

chin.en: the part of the face below the mouth

chirp.en: a short, high-pitched sound made by small birds or insects

ex.en: She woke up to the chirp of birds outside.
ex.ko: 그녀는 밖에서 새들이 짹짹거리는 소리에 잠에서 깼다.

choice.en: the act of choosing between two or more things

ex.en: It was a difficult choice to make.

choice.cue: 고를 수 있는 것들 중 하나
choice.en: one of a number of things you can choose from

ex.ko: 오늘 밤 저녁 메뉴 선택지가 많지 않다.

cigarette.en: a thin tube of paper filled with tobacco for smoking

ex.en: He stepped outside to smoke a cigarette.

citizen.en: a person who officially belongs to a particular country

ex.en: She became a citizen of Canada last year.

claim.en: say that something is true, without proof

ex.en: He claims that he saw a UFO.

claim.en: a statement that something is true

ex.en: The company denied the claim.

claim.en: ask for something because you believe it is your right

ex.en: She claimed compensation for the damage.

claim.en: a demand for something you believe you have a right to

ex.en: He filed an insurance claim after the accident.

classic.en: a book, film, or piece of art of the highest quality that has lasting value

ex.en: This novel is considered a classic of American literature.
ex.ko: 이 소설은 미국 문학의 고전으로 여겨진다.

classic.en: considered to be of the highest quality and lasting value

ex.en: It's a classic example of good design.

classic.en: typical of a particular kind of thing

ex.en: He made the classic mistake of trusting a stranger.
ex.ko: 그는 낯선 사람을 믿는 전형적인 실수를 저질렀다.

classical music.en: traditional Western music, such as symphonies, written according to established forms

ex.en: She enjoys listening to classical music while studying.
ex.ko: 그녀는 공부하면서 클래식 음악 듣는 것을 즐긴다.

cleaner.en: a machine used to clean floors or carpets, such as a vacuum cleaner

ex.en: He used the cleaner to vacuum the living room.

cleaner.en: a substance used to clean things

ex.en: She used a strong cleaner to remove the stain.
ex.ko: 그녀는 얼룩을 지우려고 강한 세제를 사용했다.

cleaner.en: a person whose job is cleaning buildings or houses

ex.en: The cleaner comes to the office every evening.

clear.en: remove things from a place to make it tidy or empty

ex.en: Please clear the table after dinner.
ex.ko: 저녁 식사 후에 식탁을 좀 치워 주세요.

ex.en: The instructions were clear and simple.

clear.en: (of weather or sky) free of clouds

ex.en: It was a clear day with a blue sky.
We had a clear view of the mountains.

clearly.en: in a way that is easy to see, hear, or understand

clearly.en: used to say that something is obviously true

clerk.en: an employee who does office work such as keeping records

ex.en: She works as a clerk in the city hall.

clerk.en: a person who works in a store, helping customers

ex.en: The clerk helped me find the right size.

click.en: a short, light sound like something snapping shut

ex.ko: 그녀는 문이 잠기며 딸깍 소리를 들었다.

ex.en: The pen clicked as she pressed the button.
ex.ko: 그녀가 버튼을 누르자 펜에서 딸깍 소리가 났다.

click.en: press a button on a computer mouse to select something

climbing.cue: 손발을 써서 높은 곳을 오르는 활동

ex.en: Rock climbing requires a lot of strength.
ex.ko: 암벽 등반은 많은 힘을 필요로 한다.

clone.en: make an exact genetic copy of a living thing

ex.en: The clone looked exactly like the original animal.
ex.ko: 그 클론은 원래 동물과 똑같이 생겼다.

clone.en: an unauthorized copy or imitation of something

ex.ko: 그 시장은 유명 브랜드의 값싼 모조품을 팔았다.

clown.en: a performer who wears funny clothes and makeup to make people laugh

ex.en: The clown made balloon animals for the kids.
ex.ko: 그 광대는 아이들에게 풍선 동물을 만들어 주었다.

clown.en: someone who behaves in a silly or foolish way

ex.en: Stop acting like a clown and take this seriously.

clue.cue: 문제를 해결하는 데 도움이 되는 정보
clue.en: a piece of information that helps you solve a problem or mystery

ex.en: The detective found an important clue at the scene.

coast.en: move smoothly and easily without using much effort or power

ex.en: The car coasted down the hill with the engine off.
ex.ko: 그 차는 엔진을 끈 채 언덕을 미끄러지듯 내려갔다.

coast.en: the land next to the sea

ex.en: They spent the summer at the coast.

coin.en: a flat, round piece of metal used as money

ex.en: He found a coin on the sidewalk.

coin.en: invent a new word or phrase

ex.en: She coined the term for this new technology.
ex.ko: 그녀는 이 새로운 기술을 위한 용어를 만들었다.

cola.en: a sweet, brown, carbonated soft drink

ex.en: Would you like a cola with your meal?
The garden was full of colorful flowers.
He has led a colorful life full of adventures.
ex.ko: 그는 모험으로 가득한 흥미로운 삶을 살아왔다.

column.en: a tall vertical structure used to support a building

ex.en: The temple was supported by stone columns.

column.en: a regular article in a newspaper or magazine giving opinions

ex.en: She writes a weekly column about food.
ex.ko: 그녀는 음식에 대한 주간 칼럼을 쓴다.

column.en: a vertical line of numbers or words in a table

ex.en: Add up the numbers in the first column.
ex.ko: 첫 번째 열에 있는 숫자들을 더하세요.

comb.en: arrange your hair using a comb

ex.en: She combed her hair before school.
ex.ko: 그녀는 학교 가기 전에 머리를 빗었다.

comb.en: a flat tool with narrow teeth used to arrange hair

ex.en: He keeps a comb in his pocket.
Police combed the area for evidence.
ex.ko: 경찰은 증거를 찾아 그 지역을 샅샅이 뒤졌다.

comfortable.en: giving physical ease and relief from pain or discomfort

ex.en: This chair is very comfortable.

comfortable.en: relaxed and free from worry or stress

ex.en: I feel comfortable talking to her about anything.
ex.ko: 나는 그녀에게 어떤 이야기든 편하게 할 수 있다.

comfortable.en: having enough money to live without financial worry

ex.en: They live a comfortable life in the suburbs.
ex.ko: 그들은 교외에서 경제적으로 여유로운 삶을 산다.

comic.en: a professional performer who tells jokes to make people laugh

ex.en: The comic had the whole audience laughing.
ex.ko: 그 코미디언은 관객 전체를 웃게 만들었다.

ex.en: The movie had several comic moments.
ex.ko: 그 영화에는 몇몇 웃기는 장면들이 있었다.

comic.en: a magazine or book that tells a story through pictures, especially for children

ex.en: He spent his allowance on comics every week.

commitment.en: a strong dedication to a cause, activity, or relationship

ex.en: Her commitment to the project impressed everyone.
ex.ko: 그 프로젝트에 대한 그녀의 헌신은 모두를 감동시켰다.

commitment.en: a promise or firm decision to do something

ex.en: I have a prior commitment that evening.

committee.en: a group of people chosen to discuss or decide something

ex.en: The committee will meet next Monday to discuss the budget.
ex.ko: 위원회는 다음 주 월요일에 예산을 논의하기 위해 모일 것이다.

common.cue: 자주 발생하거나 널리 볼 수 있는
common.en: happening or existing often; found in many places

ex.en: This is a common mistake among beginners.

common.en: shared by two or more people or things

ex.en: They share a common interest in music.
ex.ko: 그들은 음악에 대한 공통의 관심사를 가지고 있다.

communicate.en: share information or ideas with someone

ex.en: It's important to communicate clearly with your team.

communication.en: the activity of sharing information or ideas with others

ex.en: Good communication is key to a healthy relationship.

communication.en: the sending and receiving of messages, especially by phone, radio, or the internet

ex.en: The storm disrupted communication in the area.

company.en: an organization that makes or sells goods or provides a service

ex.en: She works for a large software company.

company.en: the state of being with another person or people

ex.en: I enjoy her company.
ex.ko: 나는 그녀와 함께 있는 것이 즐겁다.

company.en: a guest or guests visiting your home

ex.en: We're having company for dinner tonight.
ex.ko: 오늘 밤 저녁 식사에 손님이 온다.

comparative.en: the form of an adjective or adverb used to compare two things (e.g. 'bigger')

ex.en: 'Better' is the comparative form of 'good.'

comparative.en: relating to or based on comparison

ex.en: We did a comparative study of the two systems.
ex.ko: 우리는 두 시스템에 대한 비교 연구를 진행했다.

compare.en: examine two or more things to see how they are similar or different

ex.en: Let's compare prices before we buy.

compare.en: describe something as being similar to something else

ex.en: Critics compared her writing to that of a famous author.
ex.ko: 평론가들은 그녀의 글을 유명 작가의 글에 비유했다.

competition.en: a situation in which people or businesses try to be more successful than others

ex.en: There is a lot of competition in the smartphone market.

competition.en: an event in which people compete to win

ex.en: She entered a singing competition.

complain.en: say that you are unhappy or unsatisfied about something

ex.en: He complained about the noise all night.

complaint.en: a statement that something is unsatisfactory or unacceptable

ex.en: The hotel received several complaints about the noise.
ex.ko: 그 호텔은 소음에 대한 여러 항의를 받았다.

ex.en: He suffers from a chronic heart complaint.
ex.ko: 그는 만성 심장 질환을 앓고 있다.

ex.en: She completed the marathon in under four hours.

complete.en: having all the necessary or usual parts

ex.en: The kit includes a complete set of tools.
ex.ko: 그 키트에는 완전한 공구 세트가 들어 있다.

complete.en: write all the required information on a form

ex.en: Please complete this form and return it.
Beethoven is one of the most famous composers in history.
ex.ko: 베토벤은 역사상 가장 유명한 작곡가 중 한 명이다.

concentrate.en: direct all your attention and effort toward one thing

ex.en: Try to concentrate on your homework.

concentrate.en: make a liquid stronger or thicker by removing water

ex.en: The factory concentrates the juice before shipping it.
ex.ko: 그 공장은 배송 전에 주스를 농축시킨다.

concern.en: be about or relate to something

ex.en: This report concerns the company's finances.
ex.ko: 이 보고서는 회사의 재정에 관한 것이다.

ex.en: Her health concerns her family.

concern.en: a feeling of worry about something

ex.en: There is growing concern about climate change.
ex.ko: 기후 변화에 대한 우려가 커지고 있다.

condition.en: train a person or animal to behave in a certain way through repeated practice

ex.en: The dog was conditioned to sit when it heard the bell.
ex.ko: 그 개는 종소리를 들으면 앉도록 길들여졌다.

condition.en: the state that something or someone is in at a particular time

ex.en: The car is in excellent condition.

condition.en: something that must happen or be true before something else can happen

ex.en: You can borrow the car on one condition — fill up the tank.
ex.ko: 한 가지 조건으로 차를 빌려줄게 — 기름을 채워 놔.

confident.en: feeling sure about your own abilities or about something happening

ex.en: She felt confident about the exam.

confuse.en: make someone unable to think clearly or understand something

ex.en: The instructions confused everyone.
People often confuse the twins for each other.
ex.ko: 사람들은 종종 그 쌍둥이를 서로 혼동한다.

confused.en: unable to think clearly or understand something

ex.en: I was confused by the instructions.

consequence.en: something that happens because of a particular action or situation

ex.en: Losing his job was a direct consequence of his mistake.
ex.ko: 그가 직장을 잃은 것은 그의 실수의 직접적인 결과였다.

consider.en: think carefully about something before making a decision

ex.en: Please consider my proposal.

consider.en: think of someone or something in a particular way

ex.en: She is considered one of the best players in the world.
ex.ko: 그녀는 세계 최고의 선수 중 한 명으로 여겨진다.

consist.en: be made up of particular parts or things

ex.en: The committee consists of ten members.
ex.ko: 그 위원회는 열 명의 구성원으로 이루어져 있다.

contact.en: communicate with someone, especially by phone, email, or letter

ex.en: Please contact me if you have any questions.

contact.en: a person you can communicate with, or their phone number or email

ex.en: I saved her number in my contacts.

contact.en: the state of touching or being close to something

ex.en: Avoid contact with the chemical.

container.en: an object such as a box or jar that is used to hold things

ex.en: She stored the leftovers in a plastic container.
ex.ko: 그녀는 남은 음식을 플라스틱 용기에 보관했다.

context.en: the words or sentences that come before and after a word, helping explain its meaning

ex.en: You can guess the meaning of the word from the context.
ex.ko: 문맥을 통해 그 단어의 의미를 추측할 수 있다.

context.en: the situation or circumstances surrounding an event

ex.en: It's important to understand the historical context of the war.
ex.ko: 그 전쟁의 역사적 맥락을 이해하는 것이 중요하다.

continent.en: one of the large landmasses of the earth, such as Asia or Africa

ex.en: Africa is the second-largest continent.

continue.en: keep doing something or keep happening without stopping

ex.en: Please continue with your presentation.

contrast.en: a clear difference between two things being compared

ex.en: There is a striking contrast between the two brothers.
ex.ko: 그 두 형제 사이에는 뚜렷한 차이가 있다.

contrast.en: compare two things to show how they are different

ex.en: The teacher contrasted the two poems.

control.en: have the power to direct or influence what happens

ex.en: The pilot controls the plane's speed and direction.

control.en: the power to direct or manage something

ex.en: She lost control of the car on the icy road.
ex.ko: 그녀는 빙판길에서 차에 대한 통제력을 잃었다.

control.en: a device used to operate a machine

ex.en: He pressed the volume control on the remote.
ex.ko: 그는 리모컨에서 음량 조절 장치를 눌렀다.

convenience.en: the quality of being easy or useful for a particular purpose

ex.en: For convenience, the store is open 24 hours.
ex.ko: 편의를 위해 그 가게는 24시간 문을 연다.

convenience.en: a device or facility that makes life easier

ex.en: The apartment has modern conveniences like a dishwasher.
ex.ko: 그 아파트에는 식기세척기 같은 현대적인 편의 시설이 있다.

convenient.en: easy to use or suited to your needs

ex.en: The new bus stop is very convenient for me.
ex.ko: 새로 생긴 버스 정류장은 나에게 매우 편리하다.

cooker.en: a device or appliance used for cooking food

ex.en: She put the pot on the cooker.

cooking.en: the activity of preparing food by heating it

ex.en: Cooking is one of her favorite hobbies.
ex.ko: 요리는 그녀가 가장 좋아하는 취미 중 하나이다.

cooler.en: a container used to keep food or drinks cold

ex.en: We packed sandwiches and drinks in the cooler.

correctly.en: in a way that is right or accurate

ex.en: Make sure you spell the word correctly.
ex.ko: 그 단어의 철자를 정확하게 쓰도록 하세요.

cost.en: the amount of money needed to buy or do something

ex.en: The cost of the trip was higher than expected.
This bag costs fifty dollars.

count.en: say numbers in order, or find the total number of something

ex.en: She counted the coins in the jar.
ex.ko: 그녀는 병 안에 있는 동전을 세었다.

count.en: the total number of something, or the act of counting

ex.en: The final count showed 500 votes.
Every vote counts in this election.

country.en: an area of land with its own government and people

ex.en: France is a country in Europe.

country.en: land outside of cities and towns

ex.en: They live out in the country.

countryside.en: land in the countryside, away from towns and cities

ex.en: We drove through the beautiful countryside.

couple.en: two people who are married or in a romantic relationship

ex.en: The couple got married last summer.

couple.en: a small number of things or people, usually two or a few

ex.en: I'll be back in a couple of minutes.

court.en: a place where legal trials happen and justice is decided

ex.en: The case will go to court next month.
ex.ko: 그 사건은 다음 달에 법정에 간다.

court.en: a marked area used for playing sports like tennis or basketball

ex.en: They played tennis on the court.

crazy.en: behaving in a strange or foolish way

ex.en: That's a crazy idea.
She's crazy about horror movies.
ex.ko: 그녀는 공포 영화에 푹 빠져 있다.

ex.en: The artist created a beautiful sculpture.

creative.en: able to produce new and original ideas

ex.en: She has a very creative mind.
ex.ko: 그녀는 매우 창의적인 사고를 가지고 있다.

creativity.en: the ability to produce new and original ideas

ex.en: The competition encourages creativity among students.

creature.en: a living being, especially an animal

ex.en: The ocean is full of strange creatures.

credit.cue: 나중에 갚기로 하고 돈을 빌리는 것
credit.en: an arrangement to pay for something at a later time

ex.en: I bought the TV on credit.
ex.ko: 나는 그 TV를 신용 거래로 샀다.

credit.en: money that a bank or company allows you to borrow

ex.en: The bank increased her credit limit.

credit.en: public recognition for doing something good

ex.en: She deserves credit for her hard work.
ex.ko: 그녀는 노력에 대한 공로를 인정받을 자격이 있다.

cricket.en: a game played with a bat and ball by two teams of eleven players

ex.en: England and Australia played a cricket match.

cricket.en: a small jumping insect that makes a chirping sound

ex.en: We could hear crickets chirping outside.

crime.en: an action that is against the law

ex.en: Robbery is a serious crime.

crisp.en: a thin slice of fried potato eaten as a snack (British English)

ex.en: He ate a bag of crisps while watching TV.
ex.ko: 그는 TV를 보면서 감자칩 한 봉지를 먹었다.

crisp.en: pleasantly hard and easy to break; not soft

ex.en: The apple was fresh and crisp.

crisp.en: (of air or weather) pleasantly cold and fresh

ex.en: It was a crisp autumn morning.

criticize.en: say that someone or something is wrong or bad

ex.en: He was criticized for his decision.

cross.en: go from one side of something to the other

ex.en: Look both ways before you cross the street.

cross.en: a shape with a vertical and horizontal line crossing, especially as a Christian symbol

ex.en: She wore a small cross around her neck.
ex.ko: 그녀는 목에 작은 십자가를 걸고 있었다.

ex.en: She was cross with him for being late.

crowd.en: a large number of people gathered together

ex.en: A crowd gathered outside the stadium.

crowd.en: a group of friends who spend time together (informal)

ex.en: She hangs out with a different crowd now.
ex.ko: 그녀는 요즘 다른 친구 무리와 어울린다.

ex.en: The subway was crowded during rush hour.

crown.en: an ornamental headpiece worn by a king or queen

ex.en: The queen wore a golden crown.

crown.en: the title or position of being the champion or best

ex.en: She won the crown at the beauty pageant.

crown.en: an artificial covering placed over a damaged tooth

ex.en: The dentist put a crown on his broken tooth.
ex.ko: 치과의사는 그의 부러진 치아에 크라운을 씌웠다.

cruise.en: travel at a steady, moderate speed, especially for pleasure

ex.en: The car cruised along the highway.

cruise.en: a vacation trip taken on a large ship

ex.en: They went on a Caribbean cruise.

cupboard.en: a piece of furniture with doors and shelves used for storage

ex.en: She put the plates back in the cupboard.

curry.en: a spicy dish made with vegetables or meat and curry spices, often eaten with rice

ex.en: We ordered chicken curry for dinner.

curse.en: a magic spell intended to cause harm to someone

ex.en: The old woman put a curse on the village.

curse.en: a rude or offensive word said in anger

ex.en: He muttered a curse under his breath.

curse.en: say rude or offensive words, or wish harm on someone

ex.en: She cursed loudly when she dropped the phone.
ex.ko: 그녀는 휴대폰을 떨어뜨리고 크게 욕을 했다.

custom.en: a traditional way of behaving that is followed by a group of people

ex.en: It's a local custom to remove your shoes before entering.
ex.ko: 들어가기 전에 신발을 벗는 것이 이 지역의 관습이다.

ex.en: It was his custom to take a walk after dinner.
ex.ko: 저녁 식사 후 산책하는 것이 그의 습관이었다.

customer.en: a person who buys goods or services

ex.en: The store treats every customer with respect.
ex.ko: 그 가게는 모든 고객을 정중하게 대한다.

cycling.en: the activity or sport of riding a bicycle

ex.en: Cycling is a great way to stay fit.
ex.ko: 자전거 타기는 건강을 유지하는 좋은 방법이다.

ex.en: Reading is part of her daily routine.
The medicine should be taken daily.
He reads the daily on his way to work.

dam.en: a large wall built to hold back water and control its flow

ex.en: The dam provides electricity for the whole region.
ex.ko: 그 댐은 그 지역 전체에 전기를 공급한다.

dancer.en: a person who dances, especially professionally

ex.en: She has trained as a ballet dancer since childhood.
ex.ko: 그녀는 어릴 때부터 발레 무용수로 훈련받았다.

danger.en: the possibility of harm or injury

ex.en: The workers were warned of the danger of falling rocks.
ex.ko: 인부들은 낙석의 위험에 대해 경고를 받았다.

dangerous.en: likely to cause harm or injury

ex.en: It's dangerous to swim in that river.

daylight.cue: 해가 떠 있는 동안의 밝은 시간
daylight.en: the natural light that comes during the day

ex.en: We need to finish the hike before daylight ends.
ex.ko: 해가 지기 전에 등산을 끝내야 한다.

ex.en: The plant in the corner is dead.
I'm dead after that long shift.
ex.ko: 그 긴 근무를 마치고 나니 몹시 지쳤다.

ex.en: You're dead right about that.
ex.ko: 그것에 대해 네 말이 완전히 맞아.

deal.en: take action to handle a problem or situation

ex.en: She knows how to deal with difficult customers.
ex.ko: 그녀는 까다로운 고객을 다루는 법을 안다.

deal.en: an agreement, especially in business, that benefits both sides

ex.en: They made a deal to share the profits.

deal.en: give out playing cards to players in a game

ex.en: It's your turn to deal the cards.
The death of her grandfather was very sudden.

debate.en: a discussion in which people express different opinions about a topic

ex.en: There was a lively debate about the new policy.
ex.ko: 새 정책에 대한 활발한 토론이 있었다.

debate.en: discuss a topic formally, presenting different opinions

ex.en: The candidates debated on live television.

decide.en: choose something after thinking about the possibilities

ex.en: We haven't decided where to go on vacation yet.
ex.ko: 우리는 아직 휴가를 어디로 갈지 결정하지 못했다.
